name: volatility_asymmetry
mechanism: This factor captures the asymmetry in volatility by comparing the realized volatility to both upside and downside semivolatile measures. It highlights how much more or less volatile the asset is on the upside versus the downside.
failure_mode: If either of the semivolatility values is zero, this calculation will fail, indicating a potential issue with the data or model assumptions.

name: cross_asset_residual_behavior
mechanism: This factor examines how the target asset's residual return compares to the dispersion of returns across other assets. It provides insight into whether the target asset behaves differently from others.
failure_mode: If either the beta residual return or the cross-asset return dispersion is zero, this calculation will fail, indicating a potential issue with data consistency.

name: flow_confirmation
mechanism: This factor gauges the confirmation of flow by comparing the target asset's signed taker flow with the cross-asset agreement. It highlights when the flows are aligned and positive.
failure_mode: If either the signed taker flow or the cross-asset agreement contains outliers or errors, this calculation may not accurately reflect flow confirmation.

name: volatility_asymmetry_trend
mechanism: measures the asymmetry in upside and downside volatility to identify trend quality in the current symbol
failure_mode: if volatility is zero, division fails, indicating potential market closure or illiquidity

name: cross_asset_flow_confirmation
mechanism: identifies the dominant flow agreement across assets to confirm cross-asset liquidity behavior
failure_mode: if flow values are negative or zero, maximum fails to capture meaningful cross-asset dynamics

name: liquidity_conditioned_residual_return
mechanism: assesses residual returns relative to liquidity conditions to isolate liquidity-driven price movements
failure_mode: if liquidity ratio is zero, division fails, indicating potential liquidity exhaustion

name: volatility_range_disparity
mechanism: compares realized volatility with intrabar range to detect volatility asymmetry in price movement
failure_mode: if range exceeds volatility, subtraction yields negative values, indicating range dominance
